expectations.text: Classifies the CV screening/ranking tool as HIGH-RISK under Annex III point 4(a) (employment: recruitment or selection, incl. placing targeted job ads, analysing and filtering applications, evaluating candidates)
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Annex III, point 4 — Employment, workers' management and access to self-employment... recruitment or selection of natural persons, including: Placing targeted job advertisements, Analysing and filtering job applications, Evaluating candidates'.

expectations.text: States the compliance deadline for Annex III standalone high-risk systems as 2 December 2027 (as amended by the Digital Omnibus, adopted June 2026; originally 2 August 2026). Any deadline stated must be 2 Dec 2027, not Aug 2026.
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'High-risk AI system obligations under Annex III generally apply from 2 August 2026... most Annex III high-risk obligations from 2 August 2026.' This is the outdated pre-Digital-Omnibus deadline and directly violates the assertion's requirement that any stated deadline must be 2 Dec 2027, not Aug 2026. No mention of the Digital Omnibus or the deferred deadline anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: Walks through core provider obligations citing Arts. 9-17 (risk management, data governance, technical documentation, logging, transparency to deployers, human oversight, accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity, QMS)
expectations.evidence: Numbered list covers Art. 9 (risk management), Art. 10 (data governance), Art. 11 (technical documentation), Art. 12 (logging), Art. 13 (transparency), Art. 14 (human oversight), Art. 15 (accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity), and separately flags Art. 17 QMS as required.

expectations.text: Mentions conformity assessment and registration: self-assessment per Annex VI for Annex III point 4 systems, EU declaration of conformity (Art. 47), CE marking (Art. 48), EU database registration (Art. 49)
expectations.evidence: Response states 'this can be an internal/provider self-assessment against Annex VI... CE marking and EU declaration of conformity (Articles 47, 48)... Registration — register the system in the EU high-risk AI database... (Article 49)'.

expectations.text: Notes the Art. 6(3) derogation exists but explains it is narrow and does not apply where the system profiles natural persons / materially influences hiring decisions (profiling is always high-risk)
expectations.evidence: No mention of Art. 6(3), the narrow derogation/exception pathway, or the profiling override anywhere in the response. The classification section jumps straight to 'Yes, high-risk' without addressing or ruling out the Art. 6(3) carve-out.
